So ladies and gentlemen, at this point in the program, you are about to learn about one of the most critical things that in my opinion, if you avoid, you're going to flourish, prosper, profit, and prosper. You're going to do so much because if you avoid this one critical trap in my opinion and only do it with appropriate and under right circumstances, I think that you're going to be a winner. And this one trap that I'm referring to is partying. Now what do I mean? By partying, in my opinion of what partying means and how we're going to be contextual with this definition is we're going to relate it to things that you can do when you're young because keep in mind I'm 22 so in my opinion right now there's a lot more pitfalls and distractions if you will than what there can be in a latter part of where my lifetime would be right? So right now in my life I know that I've gone through the period where I was young or younger. I commonly get that whenever I say that to older people they go, you're still young. Anyway so from a timeline perspective, from let's say high school right, that's when I did all my diddily-dally right, that's when I did all my partying, that's when I went to my friend's golf course when I was younger, that's when I got my drinks in, that's when I did my alcoholism if you will and from that point on I actually transitioned out of that when most people would go into college I actually went into sales and I immediately went right into focusing right and at 16 I was still in business right I had a video production company so even during my partying phase I was an entrepreneur at 18 when most people transitioned into college got there freshman 15 and started binge drinking I personally had moved to Colorado and I was very focused, won't lie, I had smoked some medicinal cannabis and the week that I moved out there it actually became recreational. I'd slowed down for two years of my life however I wasn't going out drinking, I wasn't going out doing anything that in my opinion once again would have put me in a situation where I'd have to wake up at 8am after trying to get home at 5-6am like I'd seen most of my peers do I wouldn't be in a position where I had gained so much weight or put my body in an unhealthy state or put my energy levels in a position where they could be under just heavy pressure at all times I didn't put my longevity of my health in stake if you will from going out and just doing what all of the peers around me were doing right all of the people that I didn't know in Colorado were doing it. Colorado is a very alcoholic state whereas Ohio where I'd grown up everybody was just in college at the time so I'd love once again a lot of alcoholism, a lot of people going out and partying, me personally I would have long nights where I'd have whiteboard sessions where I'd be strategizing long term goals, I personally would have friends over who I'd mastermind and would sit in groups where we would talk about what we were going to do in the future, what we were doing right then and there to get better results and better actions than what we were currently doing the day before. We were still focused our long nights were long conversations about things that have actually externalized and been drawn to fruition right. There's a lot of other people that literally are my exact same age that I see and have watched and have kept an eye on growing up just for comparable and selfish reasons alone to see if I'm right and what it is that I'm giving you because once again I'm never going to talk to you in opinion so always be counseled. I'm talking to you from a perspective at 22 where I've watched, maybe you are the young person, maybe you are the older person, whoever you are there's always somebody around you who says hey let's go party or hey let's go to the bar or hey let's go stay up way late at night and not do anything productive. So once again always, always, always remember that time is more valuable than anything else and that if you are going to invest in partying be sure to do it like my man Christopher Kye taught me, the billionaire network or go to a party that has billionaires or 100 millionaires or tens of millionaires or whatever it is that you want to be around that's going to help you create relationships that will help you profit and prosper. If you are going to ever do anything with partying which I won't lie sometimes if you follow me on Snapchat at the life of Haynes you're going to find that you'll actually watch me potentially go somewhere where it might look like an environment where it's a party but in reality I'm there for an hour to meet three people strategically who I've already coordinated with prior to going that way my time is managed at that environment because I'm not there to do a little hip-boggy you know I'm not there to dance I'm there you know I might have two steps depending on who I go with but I'll tell you what the only thing that I'm doing whenever I go to a party is strategically meeting somebody in that room that I'm already aware is in that room and if I do end up in that environment from any kind of circumstance that I may be drawn into typically by females at this question. People understand together that if put into a circumstance like that I would be in the mindset to seek out somebody who looks like it could be a quality conversation seek out if it is and then if not use that as an excuse to bail out. Don't party ladies and gentlemen profit and prosper instead.
